packet.criterion_text: Objective severity trend: PASI, BSA, and/or IGA show a directional change across visits.

evidence.quote: PASI 6.3 today (6.1 on 2026-02-02 — essentially flat, within measurement noise). BSA 8%. IGA 2.

packet.reasoning: Objective indices rose sharply and progressively across dated visits: PASI 6.1 (2026-02-02) to 6.3 (2026-03-16) to 14.2 (2026-05-10) to 16.8 (2026-06-29), a cumulative rise of over 10 points and well beyond the rubric's >=2-point threshold; BSA climbed from 8% to 18% and IGA stepped up from 2 to 3. This is a clinically meaningful worsening trend documented at four encounters.

packet.criterion_text: Symptom trajectory, including nail and joint involvement, shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: onycholysis noted on two fingernails (right index and right middle), with distal separation and slight yellowish discoloration
nail plate with pitting and longitudinal ridging, mild dystrophy
few scattered erythematous papules over the antecubital arm, mild extent
erythematous scaly plaque with scale along frontal scalp hairline, localized
confluent erythematous plaques with thick scale over extensor arm, extensive
extensive scaly erythematous plaques with adherent scale across frontal scalp, severe

packet.reasoning: New nail involvement (onycholysis on two fingernails, corroborated by photographed pitting and ridging) appears on 2026-05-10 with no earlier nail documentation. The photographs also show site-level progression: arm lesions evolve from few scattered mild papules (2026-02-02) to extensive confluent thick-scaled plaques (2026-06-29), and scalp disease progresses from a localized hairline plaque (2026-05-10) to severe extensive frontal scalp involvement (2026-06-29). No joint symptoms are documented, so joint status is not asserted either way. Chart-note documentation of symptoms is thin, so photo findings were used to establish the trajectory alongside the single nursing-note quote.
packet.gap: The June visit never re-examined or documented the fingernail changes that were first noted in May.

packet.criterion_text: DLQI (Dermatology Life Quality Index) trend shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: DLQI 7, driven by residual itch and self-consciousness at the gym.
DLQI 15 today (was 8), with high scores on symptoms, feelings, and work domains.
DLQI 17. Compared with 2026-05-10 (PASI 14.2, DLQI 15), objective and quality-of-life measures are both worse.

packet.reasoning: DLQI rose from 7 (2026-02-02) to 8 (2026-03-16) to 15 (2026-05-10) to 17 (2026-06-29). The May jump of 7 points alone exceeds the rubric's >=4-point threshold, and the trend is sustained upward through June, meeting the definition of worsening quality-of-life impact.

packet.criterion_text: Treatment-response adequacy: the current regimen has been given an adequate, adherent trial.

evidence.quote: Adherence and pharmacy fill detail deferred to today's separate review by clinic staff
Summary line for the chart: adherence confirmed, no gaps, no interruptions.

packet.reasoning: The 2026-06-29 derm note deferred adherence assessment to a dedicated staff review conducted the same day, and that adherence note concludes 'adherence confirmed, no gaps, no interruptions.' On this evidence the current regimen has been taken as scheduled without interruption, so the trial is adherent; despite that adherent trial, the objective indices (criterion 1) and DLQI (criterion 3) worsened, indicating inadequate response to the current regimen. The candidates do not quote the regimen name, start date, or dosing schedule directly, which limits verification of trial duration.
packet.gap: The notes never spell out which medication the patient is on, when it was started, or its dosing schedule.

packet.criterion_text: Worsening cannot be attributed to disease if a confounder (non-adherence, infection, steroid-withdrawal or seasonal flare) is unaddressed.

evidence.quote: No infection, no steroid taper or withdrawal, and no seasonal pattern noted on review of the interval history.

packet.reasoning: The 2026-06-29 adherence review addresses all four confounder classes for the interval leading to the most recent measurements: non-adherence is excluded ('adherence confirmed, no gaps, no interruptions'), and infection, steroid taper/withdrawal, and seasonal pattern are each excluded ('No infection, no steroid taper or withdrawal, and no seasonal pattern noted'). With all named confounders excluded on interval-history review dated the same day as the latest worsening measurements, the best-case attribution is that the deterioration in PASI/BSA/IGA and DLQI reflects true disease activity.

attempts: confounder missed doses infection steroid seasonal
